diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic generalised itch with no primary rash is the hallmark of senile/xerotic pruritus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe diffuse xerosis — dry, rough, scaly, cracked skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asteatotic (dry, cracked, crazy-paving) skin is the substrate for senile pruritus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weeping golden-yellow purulent crusts in deep scratch wounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Honey-coloured crusting with purulent exudate is characteristic of impetigo/impetiginised skin.
Secondary crusting of scratch wounds is not the tense primary bullae of pemphigoid.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade fever (37.4) with an infected wound

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Secondary skin infection with early systemic response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild fever signals a systemic response; in a frail elder it lowers the threshold for same-day review.
Currently mild and isolated, but a fever with infected wounds in frailty is the sepsis safety-net trigger.
Erythema and warmth around wounds mark the impetigo-to-cellulitis boundary; extent requires in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erythema NOT rapidly spreading up the calf; no whole-leg swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised, non-advancing erythema keeps the picture at localised infection rather than emergency cellulitis.
Bilateral, non-swollen calves argue against a unilateral DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: No rigors, no new confusion, intake maintained

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of rigors, delirium and reduced intake argues against established sepsis right now.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of a primary dermatological rash before scratching

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Itch without a primary rash is the defining feature of senile/xerotic pruritus; lesions are all secondary to scratching.
No primary rash argues against pemphigoid or a primary eczematous/urticarial dermatosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperkeratotic excoriated nodules on the forearms (prurigo nodularis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Firm itchy nodules at accessible sites indicate long-standing scratching/picking.
Sharp, dirty nails cause the deep trauma and inoculate skin flora, driving the infection cycle.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal comprehensive metabolic panel (excludes systemic cause of pruritus)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Comprehensive metabolic panel / screen for systemic pruritus causes
investigation_details.expected_result: Normal — no renal, hepatic, thyroid or haematological cause
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A systemic cause of pruritus (uraemia, cholestasis, thyroid, lymphoma) could be missed; requires bloods not obtainable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Idiopathic senile pruritus is a diagnosis of exclusion; normal screening bloods support it but cannot be obtained remotely.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Secondary bacterial impetiginisation complicating senile pruritus
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Weeping crusted wounds in excoriated, xerotic skin with peri-wound erythema, driven by nail trauma, define the acute infective exacerbation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound assessment and inspection for cellulitis extent
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Cannot be done via telehealth; needed to stage the infection and decide topical versus oral antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies S. aureus/S. pyogenes and guides antibiotic choice if not resolving.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBE, CRP, U&E/creatinine (for renal antibiotic dosing), blood cultures if febrile/unwell
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses systemic involvement and provides renal function needed before dosing in a frail elder; not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical broad extra imaging (e.g. leg ultrasound for DVT) in the absence of unilateral swelling
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The bilateral superficial picture does not support routine DVT imaging; over-investigation to avoid.
